# Brand Profile: [Product/Company Name]

## Identity

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Name | [Product/Company Name] |
| Tagline | [Short tagline — under 10 words] |
| Mission | [1 sentence — what you do and why it matters] |
| Target Audience | [Who you serve — be specific about their role and knowledge level] |
| Industry | [Your space / category] |

## Voice — Who We Are

<!-- Pick 3-5 adjectives that describe how your brand sounds. -->
<!-- These drive everything else — STYLE-GUIDE.md rules and TONE-MATRIX.md shifts. -->

1. [e.g., Confident — we know our craft]
2. [e.g., Approachable — no gatekeeping, no jargon walls]
3. [e.g., Precise — we say what we mean, no fluff]
4. [e.g., Encouraging — we want users to succeed]
5. [e.g., Direct — we get to the point]

## We Sound Like

<!-- Write 2-3 sentences in your ideal voice. This is the gold standard for AI to match. -->

[Example: "We explain complex topics the way a sharp colleague would — clearly, without
talking down to you. We're opinionated when it matters and honest when we don't know.
We'd rather be helpful than impressive."]

## We Never Sound Like

<!-- Write 2-3 sentences showing what to avoid. Anti-examples are just as valuable. -->

[Example: "We never use corporate filler — no 'leveraging synergies' or 'driving value.'
We don't hedge everything with 'it depends.' We never sound like a chatbot reciting
a knowledge base."]

## Key Messages

<!-- Core themes that should appear naturally in your content. -->
<!-- These are not slogans — they're ideas your audience should take away. -->

1. [e.g., "Building with AI should feel like pair programming, not prompt engineering."]
2. [e.g., "Good tools get out of your way."]
3. [e.g., "Consistency beats cleverness."]

## Vocabulary

| Always Use | Never Use | Why |
|-----------|-----------|-----|
| [preferred term] | [avoided term] | [reason] |
| [preferred term] | [avoided term] | [reason] |
| [preferred term] | [avoided term] | [reason] |

<!-- Common patterns:
  "users" vs "customers" — depends on product type
  "build" vs "create" — depends on audience (devs vs non-devs)
  "issue" vs "problem" — tone difference
  Avoid: "leverage", "utilize", "facilitate", "synergy", "delve", "landscape"
-->
